data.title: Ingredion reports mixed Q2 results; initiates Q3 and updates FY outlook
data.description:
• Ingredion press release (NYSE:INGR): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.87 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $1833M (-2.5% Y/Y) misses by $57M.
  • Net capital expenditures were $193 million through June 30, 2025.
  • Improving guidance for full-year reported EPS to be in the range of $11.25 to $11.75 and adjusted EPS to be in the range of $11.10 to $11.60.
  • Updated Third Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Outlook The Company expects its full-year 2025 reported EPS to be in the range of $11.25 to $11.75 and adjusted EPS to be in the range of $11.10 to $11.60 (prior $10.90 to $11.60) vs. consensus of $11.31.

  • This guidance reflects tariff levels in effect as of the end of July 2025. In addition, this guidance excludes any acquisition-related integration and restructuring costs, as well as any potential impairment costs.

    The Company now expects full-year 2025 net sales to be flat, reflecting volume growth in T&HS, offset by lower price mix on pass through of lower raw material costs and expected foreign exchange impacts.

    Reported and adjusted operating income are both expected to be up mid-single-digits for full-year 2025.

    The 2025 full-year outlook further assumes the following: Texture & Healthful Solutions operating income is now expected to be up low double-digits, driven by sales volume growth; Food & Industrial Ingredients—LATAM operating income is now expected to be up low single-digits; Food & Industrial Ingredients—U.S./CAN operating income is expected to be down low single-digits; and All Other operating income is still anticipated to approach breakeven profitability.

    Corporate costs for full-year 2025 are expected to be up high single-digits, driven by IT investments and project related costs to advance our digital infrastructure.

    For full-year 2025, the Company expects both a reported and adjusted effective tax rate of 26.0% to 27.5%.

    Cash from operations for full-year 2025 is expected to be in the range of $825 million to $950 million, which includes a return to investing in working capital balances. Capital expenditures for the full year are expected to be approximately $400 to $425 million.

    For the third quarter of 2025, the Company expects net sales to be flat to up low single-digits compared to the same quarter last year, with operating income expected to be flat to down low single-digits.

data.title: Patria Investments Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 misses by $0.04
data.description:
• Patria Investments press release (NASDAQ:PAX): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 misses by $0.04.
  • Patria generated Fee Related Earnings of $46.1 million in Q2 2025, up 17% from $39.5 million in Q2 2024, with an FRE margin of 56.8%. 
  • “In 2Q we made continued progress in leveraging and expanding the diversified platform we’ve built the past several years as fundraising was a solid $1.3 billion in the quarter, bringing our total fundraising over the first half of 2025 to ~$4.5 billion. Reflecting our strong fundraising momentum and confidence in our outlook, we now expect full-year fundraising to be 5%-10% higher than our initial $6 billion target. We also reported 2Q25 FRE of $46 million, or $0.29 per share, representing year-over-year growth of 17% and 11%, respectively. Also, FEAUM grew 6% sequentially and 20% year-over-year, and we generated over $600 million of organic net inflows. Over the first half of 2025 our annualized organic growth rate exceeded 8%."
  • "While a looming trade war and global economic concerns create potential headwinds, we believe we are well positioned to generate the $200 to $225 million of FRE we are targeting for 2025 as the increased diversification of our platform is paying off in terms of fundraising and profitable organic growth, enhancing our confidence in the three-year targets we introduced at our Investor Day back on December 9th.”

data.title: Newell Brands misses top-line estimates; initiates Q3 and updates FY25 outlook
data.description:

data.title: APi Group targets $10B revenue by 2028 while expanding M&A activity and margin growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: APi Group Corporation (APG) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Russell A. Becker highlighted "record second quarter results" with net revenues increasing by 15% and organic growth over 8%. He emphasized "consistent margin expansion in Safety Services, a growing inspection, service and monitoring business, a return to organic growth in Specialty Services, record backlog in both segments; and finally, an acceleration of accretive bolt-on M&A activity."
  • Becker introduced the new long-term "10/16/60+ shareholder value creation framework" with targets of "$10 billion plus in net revenues by 2028, 16% plus adjusted EBITDA margin by 2028, 60% plus of our revenues from inspection, service and monitoring over the long term and $3 billion-plus of cumulative adjusted free cash flow through 2028."
  • The company completed six acquisitions in Q2 and seven year-to-date, including a second elevator business, and announced "several more opportunities under letter of intent."
  • Becker stated, "We remain on track to deploy approximately $250 million in accretive bolt-on M&A at attractive multiples this year."
  • CFO Glenn David Jackola reported, "Reported revenues for the 3 months ended June 30 were $2 billion, a 15% increase compared to $1.73 billion in the prior year period."
  • Jackola noted, "Adjusted EBITDA increased by 17.7% for the 3 months ended June 30, with adjusted EBITDA margin coming in at 13.7%, representing a 30 basis point increase compared to the prior year period."

Outlook

  • APi Group increased full year 2025 net revenue guidance to "$7.65 billion to $7.85 billion, up from $7.4 billion to $7.6 billion, representing organic growth in net revenues of 4% to 7% for the year."
  • Full year adjusted EBITDA guidance is now "$1.005 billion to $1.045 billion, up from $985 million to $1.035 billion, representing adjusted EBITDA growth of approximately 15% at the midpoint."
  • Q3 2025 guidance is for net revenues of "$1.985 billion to $2.035 billion," with organic revenue growth of 5% to 7%, and adjusted EBITDA of "$270 million to $280 million."
  • Jackola said, "Our increased full year revenue and EBITDA guidance is driven by updates to our business outlook, including the impact of closed M&A during the quarter, our second quarter overdelivery and our latest outlook for the rest of the year."

Financial Results

  • Net revenues for Q2 were $2 billion, up 15% year-over-year; organic growth was 8.3%.
  • Adjusted diluted earnings per share for Q2 was $0.39, an increase of $0.06 or 18.2% compared to the prior year period.
  • Safety Services revenues for Q2 were $1.36 billion (up 15.8%), with organic growth of 5.6%. Segment earnings margin rose to 17%, an 80 basis point increase.
  • Specialty Services organic revenues for Q2 were $629 million (up 13.3%), with gross margin at 18.1%, a 350 basis point decrease, and segment earnings margin at 11.3%, a 190 basis point decrease.
  • Adjusted free cash flow for the first half was $186 million, with a 40% conversion.
  • The company increased its revolving credit facility to $750 million, extended maturity to 2030, and ended the quarter with a net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio of approximately 2.2x.

Q&A

  • Timothy Michael Mulrooney, William Blair: Asked about revenue outperforming guidance and margin pressure in Specialty. Jackola responded, "We saw really strong contract and project activity across both of the segments during the second quarter... Rising material costs and the impact of weather did play a role on our margins in the quarter."
  • Andrew John Wittmann, Baird: Inquired about the nature of the guidance increase. Jackola explained, "You can think of our EBITDA raise as 1/3 of it driven by our Q2 over delivery, maybe 1/3 of it due to M&A in the quarter and maybe 1/3 of it due to an increase or an improvement in our second half business outlook."
  • Julian C.H. Mitchell, Barclays: Asked about Safety business growth rates and elevator market share. Jackola indicated, "We continue to target mid- to upper single-digit revenue growth in the service side of the business, low to mid-single digit on the project to get to that mid-single-digit, 5% to 6% revenue growth in the back half of the year."
  • Jonathan E. Tanwanteng, CJS Securities: Queried on elevator acquisition margin profile. Becker replied it is "really closer to fleet average... with the potential to go and improve."
  • Jasper James Bibb, Truist Securities: Sought color on Specialty projects pipeline and margin outlook. Jackola said, "I'd expect high single-digit organic revenue growth in the Specialty business in the third quarter."
  • Tomohiko Sano, JPMorgan: Asked about inspection business margin improvements and technology use. Jackola stated, "We continue to be able to capture low to mid-single-digit pricing in our inspection service and monitoring revenue streams... our expectation on all of our revenue streams is that we're going to continue to be able to expand margin into '26, '27 and '28."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on the sustainability of growth, margin pressures in Specialty Services, and M&A discipline, with a generally positive but probing tone, seeking specifics on outlook improvements and margin levers.
  • Management maintained a confident and constructive tone, with Becker repeatedly expressing optimism about business momentum, stating, "We are confident in our leaders' ability to execute our strategy and deliver against our 2025 plan."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, current analyst sentiment is more positive, reflecting surprise at outperformance and raised guidance. Management's tone remains highly confident during both prepared remarks and Q&A, increasingly forward-looking.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Full year net revenue and adjusted EBITDA guidance were both raised from the prior range, with organic growth targets increased from 2%-5% to 4%-7%.
  • Backlog reached a record high above $4 billion, compared to $3.5 billion in Q1.
  • Q2 saw a return to organic growth in Specialty Services, following a decline in Q1.
  • Margin expansion continued in Safety Services, while Specialty Services gross margin declined due to increased project starts and material costs.
  • M&A activity accelerated, with six acquisitions in Q2 versus targets articulated in Q1.
  • Management’s confidence and forward-looking focus increased compared to Q1, with more explicit long-term targets and strategic clarity.

Risks and Concerns

  • Rising material costs and weather were cited as key challenges, particularly impacting Specialty gross margins.
  • Management discussed potential impacts from tariffs and inflation, but indicated that mitigation strategies, such as contractual provisions and disciplined project selection, are in place.
  • Execution risks remain around integrating acquisitions and delivering on elevated backlog margins.
  • Labor availability and efficiency were mentioned as ongoing operational challenges, with technology and AI initiatives aimed at offsetting workforce constraints.

Final Takeaway

APi Group delivered record Q2 results with strong top line growth, improved margin performance in Safety Services, and a return to organic growth in Specialty Services. The company raised its full year revenue and EBITDA guidance, driven by robust backlog, accelerating M&A, and positive momentum in inspection, service, and monitoring businesses. Management reiterated its commitment to disciplined capital deployment and outlined new long-term growth targets, projecting $10 billion-plus net revenues and 16%+ adjusted EBITDA margin by 2028. The outlook remains positive, supported by a strong balance sheet, ongoing free cash flow generation, and a focused strategy to navigate macroeconomic uncertainties.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Gold faces third weekly loss; industrial metals slide amid U.S. trade moves
data.description:
Gold prices rose on Friday but were on track for a third straight weekly loss as a stronger dollar crimped safe-haven demand, while industrial metals posted losses across the board this week as markets digested a combination of US trade moves with sluggish demand in Mainland China.

Strong economic data out of the US, alongside the US-EU trade deal announcement, saw the US dollar index rise to a 2-month high just shy of the 100.0 mark, putting downward pressure on gold.

Following the US Fed’s decision to hold rates in July, Fitch's BMI said its macro team continues to expect a cumulative 50bps of cuts before the end of the year, which should support bullion as a non-yielding asset.

With further tariff announcements also likely, it expects gold prices to remain elevated and maintain the 2025 forecast of $3,100/oz.

Meanwhile, benchmark copper prices on the London Metal Exchange slumped 2.3% to $9,698/tonne after reports emerged that Chile – the world’s largest copper miner - was attempting to secure an exemption to Trump’s 50% tariff before its introduction on August 1.

Copper traded in New York plunged more than 20% on July 30 after the White Houseannounced that the 50% tariff would not apply to imports of refined copper.

"Other base metals also performed poorly this week, with aluminium and nickel prices falling 1.9% and 3.5% w-o-w to close at $2,601/tonne and $15,021/tonne, respectively, as Chinese demandcontinued to disappoint," the report said.

Ferrous metals also ended the week lower after China’s official manufacturing PMI registered the fourth consecutive month in contraction, falling to 49.3 in July from 49.7 in June. 

Elsewhere, soybean oil has outperformed the rest of the vegetable oils complex over he first half of 2025, the research by BMI said. 

On a year-to-date basis up to July 29, CBOT-listed second-month soybean oil futures posted a 42% increase.
